record.field: Senior cardiologist with 15 years experience. Specialises in interventional cardiology and heart failure management.
Currently on annual leave until end of month.
Main general medicine ward serving adult patients. Equipped with nurse call systems and standard monitoring.
Critical care unit with 24/7 monitoring, ventilators and advanced life support equipment.
Pre and post-operative surgical ward. Patients admitted for elective and emergency surgical procedures.
Labour, delivery and postnatal care. Includes labour rooms, delivery suites and postnatal beds.
Dedicated ward for patients aged 0–16 years. Child-friendly environment with play area.
Covering critical care cases in ICU.
Metformin 1000mg BD, Lisinopril 10mg OD, Aspirin 81mg OD

Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus in 2015. Currently managed with oral hypoglycaemics. Last HbA1c: 7.4% (May 2026). Known hypertension since 2018, well-controlled on Lisinopril.

No prior hospitalisations. Annual eye and foot exams up to date.
Cetirizine 10mg OD PRN, Salbutamol inhaler PRN

History of allergic rhinitis since childhood. Mild intermittent asthma, well-controlled. Anaphylaxis episode to Penicillin in 2018 — carries EpiPen. Skin prick tests confirm multiple environmental allergens.
Type 1 Diabetes, Hypertension, Mild CKD (Stage 2), Hyperlipidaemia
Insulin Glargine 28 units nocte, Insulin Aspart TDS with meals, Amlodipine 5mg OD, Atorvastatin 40mg OD
Father — MI at age 60; Mother — T2DM; Brother — hypertension

Long-standing Type 1 Diabetes on insulin therapy. Multiple complications: peripheral neuropathy (bilateral), early diabetic nephropathy. Hypertension managed pharmacologically. Annual cardiology review. Last eGFR 68 ml/min/1.73m².
Healthy child. Vaccinations up to date per UAE schedule. Mild seasonal allergic rhinitis managed conservatively. No chronic conditions.
Admitted May 2026 for laparoscopic cholecystectomy due to symptomatic gallstones. Procedure uneventful. Discharged 3 days post-op in stable condition. Follow-up scheduled in 6 weeks.
Ischaemic Heart Disease, Atrial Fibrillation, Hyperlipidaemia
Warfarin 5mg OD, Atorvastatin 80mg OD, Bisoprolol 5mg OD, Ramipril 5mg OD
Father — fatal MI at 58; Brother — stents at 52
Known IHD post-CABG 2019. AF diagnosed 2021 on anticoagulation. Regular cardiology follow-up. Last echo: EF 48%. INR target 2.0–3.0. Lipids well controlled on Atorvastatin.
Fluticasone inhaler 50mcg BD, Salbutamol inhaler PRN
Metformin 500mg TDS, Enalapril 10mg OD, Simvastatin 20mg nocte
Follow-up for diabetes management. Check HbA1c and BP. Review medications.
Allergy review. Symptoms well controlled. Prescribed antihistamines for seasonal exacerbation.
Urgent review — reported chest tightness this morning. ECG ordered.
Routine annual pediatric check-up and vaccination review.
Cardiology 6-month follow-up post-CABG. Stable. Echo and INR reviewed.
Routine diabetes and hypertension review. Fasting labs requested.
New patient — referral from GP for recurrent migraines.
Asthma review. Assess inhaler technique and check peak flow diary.
No fasting required. Avoid strenuous exercise 24h before.
Venous blood — fluoride oxalate tube (grey top)
Fast for a minimum of 8 hours. Water is permitted.
pH 4.5–8.0 | Protein: negative | Glucose: negative | WBC: <5/HPF
Collect mid-stream urine first thing in the morning. Clean-catch technique.
ALT 7–56 U/L | AST 10–40 U/L | ALP 44–147 U/L | Bilirubin 3–20 μmol/L
Fast for 8–12 hours. Avoid alcohol for 24h prior.
Clear lung fields. Normal cardiac silhouette. No consolidation, effusion or pneumothorax.
Remove jewellery and metallic objects. Wear hospital gown. Inform if pregnant.
Electrical recording — electrodes on chest and limbs
Normal sinus rhythm 60–100 bpm. Normal axis. No ST changes or conduction abnormalities.
Lie still during recording. Avoid caffeine 2h before.
HbA1c slightly above target (target <7.0%). Indicates suboptimal glycaemic control over the past 3 months. Recommend medication review and dietary counselling. Retest in 3 months.

Atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular rate (~72 bpm). No new ST-segment or T-wave changes compared to prior ECG (Dec 2025). No evidence of acute ischaemia. Rate control appears adequate on current Bisoprolol dose.

WBC 6.8 ×10³/μL — within normal limits. Eosinophil count mildly elevated at 0.6 ×10³/μL (ref <0.5), consistent with allergic state. Hb 13.2 g/dL (normal). Platelets 245 ×10³/μL (normal). No haematological cause for concern.
Ordered as part of diabetes annual review. Statin monitoring.
Take Metformin with meals to reduce GI side effects. Monitor blood glucose daily. Report any muscle pain immediately (Statin side effect).
Take in the morning. Monitor blood pressure weekly.
Avoid known allergens. Carry EpiPen at all times. If symptoms worsen seek emergency care immediately.
Dispensed full course. Patient counselled on EpiPen technique.
Take once daily at bedtime. May cause drowsiness.
Use spacer device. Shake well before use. Max 8 puffs per day.
Urgent cardiac review. Troponin results pending. Monitor closely. If chest pain recurs, transfer to ICU immediately.
Chew and swallow immediately. Do not crush.
Spray under tongue if chest pain occurs. Sit down first. Repeat after 5 min if no relief (max 3 doses). Call emergency if no relief after 3 doses.
Warfarin dose adjusted — new dose 5mg OD. INR check in 1 week. Avoid grapefruit juice. Report any unusual bleeding.
Dispensed 6-month supply. Patient given warfarin card and bleeding precautions leaflet.
Take at same time each day. INR monitoring required. Avoid NSAIDs.
Do not stop abruptly. Take in the morning.
To be finalised after LFT results. Current medications to be reviewed.
